skill.name: image-to-3d
skill.description: Image-to-3D asset creation for agents through Image Skill's hosted runtime. It guides agents to create GLB-style 3D mesh assets from owned input images, track hosted jobs, retrieve asset URLs, review costs, and submit feedback through the Image Skill CLI and API.
skill.summary: Create hosted 3D mesh assets from input images through the Image Skill CLI and API.
skill.author: danielgwilson

security_audit.summary: The scanner's Ruby backtick and weak cryptography detections are false positives caused by Markdown formatting and ordinary description text. The skill still has medium risk because it instructs agents to execute a latest npm package, contact hosted Image Skill endpoints, and optionally write agent state under hidden home directories.

medium_findings.title: Remote npm Package Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs agents to run npx with image-skill@latest and skills installer commands. This is a legitimate workflow for the skill, but it executes code fetched from npm and should be treated as supply-chain sensitive.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command blocks clearly run npx packages, including an @latest package version. The intent appears functional rather than malicious, so the concern is supply-chain risk rather than confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: Hosted API and User Media Transfer
medium_findings.description: The skill points agents to Image Skill web and API endpoints and describes hosted media URLs, jobs, receipts, payments, and feedback. This external service use is central to the skill, but users should understand that input images and generated assets may leave the local workspace.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The hosted URLs and API contract are explicit, and the skill purpose requires network processing. No evidence found of secret exfiltration or deceptive endpoints.

medium_findings.title: Hidden Agent State Directory Writes
medium_findings.description: The fallback install path tells agents to set HOME to a workspace .agent-home path and CODEX_HOME to .codex before installing the skill. This can be reasonable for isolated setup, but it writes hidden agent profile state and should be visible to users.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The filesystem writes are explicitly documented and scoped to a workspace path. The risk is moderate because hidden state can affect later agent behavior, but no stealth or persistence mechanism is present.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick and Weak Crypto Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The Ruby shell backtick findings are Markdown inline code and fenced command examples, not Ruby execution. The weak cryptography hits occur in normal description text and do not reference cryptographic algorithms or code.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed file is Markdown and YAML front matter, not Ruby source. The flagged weak-crypto locations contain prose fields, with no cryptographic implementation or algorithm selection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Latest-Version CLI Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The command examples use npx with image-skill@latest. Running latest packages can change behavior without a pinned version and depends on npm package integrity.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The npx command and @latest version are directly visible in the command block. This is a known supply-chain risk pattern, although no malicious package behavior is proven.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Hosted Runtime Dependency
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill depends on image-skill.com and api.image-skill.com for contracts and hosted API behavior. This creates network, privacy, availability, and policy dependency risk.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The external endpoints are explicit and central to the skill description. No evidence found that the endpoints are malicious, so the risk remains operational and privacy related.

dangerous_patterns.title: Hidden Workspace Home Configuration
dangerous_patterns.description: The instructions redirect HOME and CODEX_HOME to hidden workspace directories before installing the skill. This should be disclosed because it can change where agent configuration is stored.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The hidden path setup is plainly documented and not concealed. It is still security relevant because agent configuration files can influence future tool behavior.

content.value_statement: Teams need a repeatable way to turn product photos or concept images into reusable 3D assets. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Image Skill's hosted image-to-3D workflow.
actual_capabilities: Guides agents to create 3D mesh assets from a single owned input image.
Uses the Image Skill CLI guide flow before spending credits.
Requests GLB-style output through the fal.trellis-image-to-3d model.
Returns hosted job, asset, receipt, and recovery information through JSON output.
Provides install commands for Codex-compatible skill environments.
Includes a feedback path when the hosted runtime lacks a needed capability.
limitations: Requires access to the external Image Skill hosted service.
May spend Image Skill credits when creation commands are approved.
Depends on the quality, ownership, and suitability of the input image.
Does not run a local image-to-3D model or avoid network transfer.

use_cases.title: Prototype Product Assets
use_cases.description: Turn a product photo into an initial 3D mesh for review, mockups, or asset planning.

use_cases.title: Create Game Placeholder Models
use_cases.description: Generate draft GLB-style objects from concept art before commissioning final production models.

use_cases.title: Build Commerce Visualization Drafts
use_cases.description: Create hosted model assets from catalog imagery for early interactive shopping experiments.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic 3D Asset
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request with one owned image.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use image-to-3d to create a 3D asset from my uploaded product image. Start with the no-spend guide and show the command before spending.

prompt_templates.title: Check Cost Before Creation
prompt_templates.prompt: Use image-to-3d for this object photo. Confirm the estimated cost, required inputs, and generated asset format before running any paid command.

prompt_templates.title: Recover a Prior Job
prompt_templates.scenario: Continuing work from hosted job data.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use image-to-3d to recover the previous Image Skill job details and summarize the hosted asset URL, receipt, and next available actions.

prompt_templates.title: Evaluate Workflow Gaps
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review when a model or policy need is missing.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use image-to-3d to attempt the guide flow for this input. If the model cannot meet the requirement, prepare feedback with expected behavior, actual behavior, and the missing capability.

output_examples.input: A user provides a clean photo of a small consumer product and asks for a 3D model.
output_examples.output: The agent runs the no-spend guide, confirms the next runnable command, requests approval for paid creation, and returns a hosted model asset link with receipt details.

output_examples.input: A team wants a draft 3D object from concept art for a design review.
output_examples.output: The agent explains input requirements, uses the hosted image-to-3D model, and summarizes the generated asset, cost, and recovery information.

output_examples.input: The hosted model cannot satisfy a requested mesh or policy constraint.
output_examples.output: The agent stops the creation path, records the attempted command, and prepares Image Skill feedback describing the missing capability.

best_practices: Use only images you own or have permission to process through a hosted service.
Run the guide command first and confirm cost before any paid generation.
Store returned job, asset, receipt, and recovery details with the project.
anti_patterns: Do not upload confidential images without approval for external processing.
Do not run latest-version CLI commands in restricted environments without review.
Do not assume the generated mesh is production-ready without visual inspection.

faq.question: Does this skill run image-to-3D locally?
faq.answer: No. It uses the hosted Image Skill runtime and its API-backed model workflow.

faq.question: Can it create GLB-style assets?
faq.answer: Yes. The documented model path targets image-to-3D mesh output such as GLB assets.

faq.question: Does it require provider API keys?
faq.answer: No. The skill describes a hosted Image Skill identity instead of user-supplied provider credentials.

faq.question: Will it spend money automatically?
faq.answer: It should start with a no-spend guide and only run paid commands after explicit approval.

faq.question: What input works best?
faq.answer: Use a clear owned image with the target object visible and minimal visual ambiguity.

faq.question: What are the main security considerations?
faq.answer: Review external API use, npm package execution, hosted media handling, and hidden agent state paths.
